commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=01c1d666b5821c31e7431e748e3d0e53045ab82d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `extractToolResultText()` and uses it across OpenAI Responses, OpenAI Completions, Anthropic, Google/Gemini, Mistral, and active transport tests to serialize structured non-image tool-result blocks as model-visible text.

PR surface: Source +68, Tests +376. Total +444 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main and v2026.6.10 filter tool results to text blocks and fall back to `(see attached image)` when structured non-image blocks leave no text. I did not run a live provider/session repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual follow-up is needed because an automated repair cannot supply the contributor's missing real behavior proof, and maintainers should decide whether to remove or productize the structured-output cap before merge.

target.title: fix(llm): preserve structured tool results as text across providers
target.body:
## Summary
- Preserve structured non-image tool result blocks as text instead of collapsing them to `(see attached image)`.
- Share the normalization through `extractToolResultText()` across OpenAI Completions, OpenAI Responses, Google/Gemini, Anthropic, and Mistral conversion paths.
- Cover active transport paths for OpenAI Responses and Anthropic, plus provider conversion paths and focused regressions.

## What Problem This Solves
Tool results containing structured non-image blocks (JSON payloads, resource links, etc.) were being collapsed into the `(see attached image)` fallback because the conversion paths only handled image and text blocks. This caused tool output to become invisible to the model, leading to degraded tool-call chains and incorrect tool-result handling across Anthropic, Mistral, OpenAI Completions, and Google/Gemini providers.

## Review Follow-Up
- Fixed the TypeScript blocker by widening `extractToolResultText()` to accept `readonly unknown[]` and narrowing internally.
- Removed unsafe `Record<string, unknown>[]` casts from provider call sites.
- Fixed the OpenAI Responses regression test scope by moving it under `convertResponsesMessages`, using `testAllowedToolCallProviders`, and casting the intentional structured-content fixture at the boundary.
- Added direct regression coverage for active OpenAI Responses and Anthropic transport conversion.
- Expanded same-root coverage to Anthropic and Mistral provider conversions.
- Resolved CI lint/type issues: unused imports, single-line if/continue braces, `no-base-to-string` guards via `typeof` checks, shadowed `context` variable warnings, and type-cast boundaries in tests.

## Evidence
- `git diff --check` passes clean.
- Focused local Vitest gates (test runner not available in lightweight worktree; GitHub Actions provides authoritative CI).
- GitHub Actions CI is the authoritative gate for this PR (`check-prod-types`, `check-lint`, `check-test-types`, `checks-node-compact-large-2`).

## Scope And Limitations
This PR fixes the structured non-image tool-result conversion failure: blocks such as JSON/resource payloads are serialized as text before provider/transport payload creation instead of being treated like image-only tool results.

This PR does not claim to fully resolve every failure mode described in #96857. The prompt-history truncation plus provider-failover/session-corruption path described in later issue evidence is broader than this conversion fix and should remain open for follow-up investigation if it reproduces after this change.

Image-only tool results intentionally keep the existing `(see attached image)` fallback when the target model/provider cannot accept image input.

## Relationship To Prior Work
This updates and broadens the earlier provider-only direction in #96895 by adding type-safe helper boundaries, fixing test issues, covering active transport paths, and documenting the remaining scope boundary.

Refs #96857
target.author: snowzlmbot

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
